summary: Provision new ECS clusters, task definitions, and services. Every stand-up routes to an engineer for review — day-2 operations on existing ECS resources land in a later wave.

operations.title: Provision a new ECS cluster
operations.summary: Request a brand-new ECS cluster; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new ECS cluster as a complete specification: name, Container Insights observability, and tags. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: cluster_name
params.label: Cluster name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, like app-tier-cluster. Names the cluster in AWS and in the generated draft.

params.name: container_insights
params.label: Container Insights
params.help: Enhanced CloudWatch observability (CPU/memory/network per service and task) for everything running on this cluster.

params.name: container_insights_setting_name
params.label: Container Insights setting key
params.help: Fixed setting key — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: execute_command_logging
params.label: ECS Exec session logging
params.help: How ECS Exec (interactive container debugging) sessions are logged. DEFAULT uses the account's CloudWatch/S3 defaults.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this cluster does
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this cluster.

decisions: Capacity providers (Fargate, Fargate Spot, or an Auto Scaling group-backed EC2 provider) are attached as an engineer follow-up
Service Connect namespace defaults and KMS-backed execute-command encryption are engineer decisions

operations.title: Provision a new ECS task definition
operations.summary: Request a brand-new ECS task definition; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new task definition as a complete specification: launch compatibility, sizing, network mode, and IAM roles. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block — including the container spec — to review and finish. Nothing is applied without review.

params.name: family
params.label: Family
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, like checkout-api. Groups revisions of this task definition together.

params.name: container_definitions
params.label: Container definitions
params.help: The JSON container spec — image, port mappings, environment, log configuration — an engineer authors this to match the workload's image and runtime needs.

params.name: requires_compatibility
params.label: Launch type
params.help: Which ECS launch type this task definition supports.

params.name: network_mode
params.label: Network mode
params.help: awsvpc is required for Fargate and gives every task its own elastic network interface.

params.name: cpu
params.label: Task CPU units
params.help: Total vCPU units for the task (1024 = 1 vCPU).

params.name: memory
params.label: Task memory (MiB)
params.help: Total memory for the task. Must be a combination Fargate accepts for the chosen CPU.

params.name: execution_role
params.label: Execution role
params.help: The role ECS assumes to pull the image and write logs — required for Fargate tasks.

params.name: task_role
params.label: Task role
params.help: The role the CONTAINERS assume at runtime. Leave blank if the task needs no AWS API access.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this task definition does
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this task definition.

decisions: The container spec (image, port mappings, environment, log driver) is an engineer decision, matched to the workload's built image
CPU architecture / operating system family (runtime_platform), volumes, and placement constraints are engineer follow-ups
A CPU/memory combination outside Fargate's accepted pairs is caught at apply time — the engineer reviewing confirms the pair is valid

operations.title: Provision a new ECS service
operations.summary: Request a brand-new ECS service; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new service as a complete specification: the cluster and task definition it runs, desired count, network placement, and an optional load balancer target group. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: service_name
params.label: Service name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, like checkout-api-svc. Names the service in AWS and in the generated draft.

params.name: cluster
params.label: Cluster
params.help: The ECS cluster this service runs on. Create the cluster first (ecs-cluster-create) if none exists yet.

params.name: task_definition
params.label: Task definition
params.help: The task definition this service runs. Create the task definition first (ecs-task-definition-create) if none exists yet.

params.name: launch_type
params.label: Launch type
params.help: FARGATE runs tasks on serverless capacity; EC2 runs them on the cluster's registered container instances.

params.name: desired_count
params.label: Desired count
params.help: How many copies of the task this service keeps running.

params.name: subnets
params.label: Subnets
params.help: Where tasks launch. Pick subnets spanning at least two Availability Zones for resilience.

params.name: security_groups
params.label: Security groups
params.help: Who can reach this service's tasks. Leave blank to use the VPC default security group.

params.name: assign_public_ip
params.label: Assign public IP
params.help: Off for the estate's internal-only tasks. Only turn on for a task in a public subnet with no NAT.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Leave blank for a service with no load balancer. Picking one requires the container name and port below too.

params.name: lb_container_name
params.label: Container name
params.help: Must match a container name inside the task definition's container spec.

params.name: lb_container_port
params.label: Container port
params.help: The port on the container the target group forwards traffic to.

params.name: enable_execute_command
params.label: Enable ECS Exec
params.help: Turns on interactive container debugging (ecs execute-command) for this service's tasks.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this service does
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this service.

decisions: Deployment circuit breaker, deployment strategy (rolling/blue-green), and capacity provider strategy are engineer decisions
Service Connect / service discovery registration is an engineer follow-up after creation
Multiple load balancer target groups or an ALB advanced (weighted / blue-green) configuration are engineer decisions — this form wires one target group
